target_need: Coding agents and developers need one reliable local Launchdeck lifecycle control plane that is usable through a backward-compatible CLI, a canonical Agent Skill, typed local stdio MCP operations, and separately packaged Codex and Claude Plugins without duplicate services, unsafe process control, divergent state, or host-specific safety drift.

in: A reusable Application Kernel and Operation Registry that own execution, safety policy, ownership checks, compatibility, recovery, and normalized semantics
Explicit project and host scope resolution for every operation
Operation IDs, locks, a durable operation journal, effects certainty, provenance, recovery, and reconciliation
A backward-compatible CLI adapter that preserves CLI schemaVersion 1 and adds a capabilities operation
A typed local stdio MCP adapter for capabilities, diagnosis, bounded observation, configured-project low-risk lifecycle mutations, reconciliation, and digest-bound safe clean
Read-only adoption inspection without configuration mutation or implicit task start
One canonical reference-driven launchdeck-agent Skill with MCP-first routing and safety-preserving CLI fallback
One compatibility manifest and independently generated Codex and Claude Plugin packages containing a prebuilt ESM MCP runtime
Plugin install, update, disable, uninstall, and shared-state lifetime safety
Operation-contract, CLI/MCP semantic-equivalence, real stdio, fault-injection, Skill tool-sequence, host, update, uninstall, and platform verification
out: Automatic adoption or configuration writes in the first release
Medium, high, or destructive public MCP execution in the first release
Force-stop, project removal, risky clean, reset, arbitrary shell, env, cwd, force, model-authored approval fields, or permanent follow tools
Termination of external or unknown-owned processes
Remote Streamable HTTP MCP, OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI, TUI, production or remote service management
Native executable packaging and enterprise managed-policy implementation
deferred: adoption.apply after a separately reviewed plan-digest and confirmation contract
Medium-risk public MCP execution after target hosts prove reliable human elicitation
Remote MCP, enterprise policy distribution, hooks, UI, and native executable packaging

constraints: F:/github/launchdeck is the locked implementation target and current behavior authority.
The existing daemonless user-scoped Launchdeck control plane remains authoritative.
CLI, MCP, Skill, and Plugin runtimes share LAUNCHDECK_HOME, registry, run index, locks, logs, events, and operation journal.
Application Kernel policy is the only mutation authority; MCP annotations and host approvals are defense in depth only.
CLI schemaVersion 1 remains backward compatible while Agent protocol versions independently.
First-release MCP is local stdio, operates only on configured projects, and executes only operations whose current Kernel-evaluated risk is low.
Public Agent tools do not accept arbitrary command, env, cwd, force, risky cleanup, reset, or model-authored confirmation fields.
Node.js 20+ is a declared prerequisite; Plugins do not require a global CLI, npx, or install scripts.
Codex and Claude Plugins are separate generated artifacts from one compatibility manifest and build identity.
MCP-server and Plugin-host lifetime never owns managed-task lifetime.
Unknown or external process ownership is inspect-only.
No readiness claim may exceed its exact surface, host, operating-system, update, uninstall, and recovery evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: For every shared operation contract case, CLI and MCP produce semantically equivalent operation, outcome, resource, effects, safety, error, next-action, and provenance data while preserving the existing CLI envelope.
AC-002: A Plugin-started service survives host and MCP exit, remains visible from standalone CLI, and can be safely stopped from that CLI when ownership and compatibility checks pass.
AC-003: Repeating start for the same configured project/task returns the existing run with reusedExistingRun=true and does not create a second process.
AC-004: A port or PID with unknown or external ownership can be inspected but every mutation is refused without Launchdeck ownership proof.
AC-005: Public MCP refuses medium, high, destructive, arbitrary-shell, external-process, and model-authored-confirmation execution even when a host annotation or approval suggests otherwise.

AC-006: Every mutation has an operation ID persisted before side effects; after a transport failure the caller can retrieve/reconcile by a known ID or use bounded operation.list correlation when the response carrying the ID was lost, and an indeterminate operation is never automatically retried or replayed through another surface.

AC-007: Reuse of an existing operation ID with the same input digest returns or reconciles the original result; a mismatched digest or expired/missing recovery record is refused without new execution.
AC-008: Safe clean executes only after the Kernel recomputes the target set and matches a current plan digest; preview drift is refused.
AC-009: Every MCP operation resolves project context explicitly and returns a missing, ambiguous, or scope-violation error rather than defaulting to a Plugin installation directory.

AC-010: Plugin update, disable, and uninstall leave managed services, registry, journal, logs, events, and shared configuration intact; a compatible newer Plugin and standalone CLI observe the same run ID, PID, port, and state home and can safely stop the older run without duplication.
AC-011: Capabilities reports actual runtime, protocol, state, schema, component digests, supported operations, risk limits, host, and project scope; component mismatch fails writes closed.

AC-012: The canonical Skill selects MCP first only for supported safe operations, uses CLI fallback only for unavailable or unsupported safe transport, and never falls back after a business refusal or uncertain mutation.

AC-013: Codex Skill installation checks both legacy ~/.codex/skills and current $HOME/.agents/skills locations, reports identical duplicates or divergence, and never overwrites or deletes divergent user content automatically.
AC-014: A real stdio protocol client verifies initialize, tools/list, tools/call, structured output, schema errors, stdout purity, stderr diagnostics, interruption, restart, and concurrency.

AC-015: Separate real Codex and Claude host smokes verify installation, routing, project scope, MCP launch, update, disable/uninstall, cross-version run identity, and shared-state behavior on every platform claimed ready.

AC-016: adoption.inspect returns a bounded proposed configuration plan without writing project or user configuration, registering a project, starting a task, or authorizing a later adoption.apply operation.

decisions.decision: Use one shared Application Kernel and Operation Registry with thin CLI and MCP adapters.
Keep CLI schemaVersion 1 backward compatible and version the Agent protocol independently.
Limit first-release public MCP execution to configured-project operations whose current Kernel-evaluated risk is low; retain local stdio as the only transport.
Retain one canonical intent-driven launchdeck-agent Skill with MCP-first routing and guarded CLI fallback.
Generate distinct Codex and Claude Plugin artifacts from one compatibility manifest and build identity; bundle a prebuilt ESM stdio runtime and require Node.js 20+.

The Agent Operation Result uses exact top-level fields protocolVersion, operation, outcome, resource, effects, safety, error, nextActions, and provenance; outcome.kind and resource.status are separate semantics.

Store the versioned journal under LAUNCHDECK_HOME; persist Kernel-issued operation IDs before side effects, provide bounded journal correlation when a response loses the ID, keep unresolved or indeterminate entries until reconciliation, retain terminal full records for 30 days by default, and treat a supplied recovery ID with no retained record as a refusal rather than a new execution.

Skill compatibility is non-destructive: scan legacy and current Codex locations, compare canonical digests, prefer the current location for new installation, and require an explicit user action for divergent migration or removal.
No substantive visual, TUI, GUI, or layout direction is included; CLI and MCP changes are behavioral and protocol contracts that reuse existing presentation conventions.
Readiness is layered by operation contract, CLI, stdio MCP, Skill behavior, host, update/uninstall, recovery, and operating system; evidence does not roll up across untested layers.

capability_operations.signal: Kernel operation registry and normalized result contract
capability_operations.operation: Execute declared Launchdeck operations through one Kernel-owned registry and normalized result contract.
Resolve projectRef, explicit projectRoot, host-provided project root, and registered user-scope projects with deterministic precedence and scope errors.
Evaluate current configured risk, ownership proof, project scope, and version compatibility before every mutation.
Journal each mutation by Kernel-issued operation ID and input digest, recover known work, and reconcile uncertain effects without blind replay.
Route current CLI commands through the Kernel while preserving schemaVersion 1 and independently exposing capabilities.
Expose typed Agent tools generated or validated from declared operations over stdout-pure local stdio MCP.
Expose capabilities, diagnosis, project/task inspection, status, and cursor/limit-bounded logs and events without arbitrary paths or permanent follow calls.
Start, stop, restart, and run only declared configured tasks whose current Kernel policy is low risk and whose ownership/scope checks pass.
Inspect/reconcile known operation IDs and execute safe clean only from a freshly revalidated Kernel plan digest.
Inspect candidate adoption configuration and return a plan without writing configuration, registering a project, authorizing apply, or starting a task.

capability_operations.signal: Canonical Skill routing and safe fallback
capability_operations.operation: Map lifecycle intent to declared operations, observe before mutation, refuse unsafe raw control, and apply the supported MCP-to-CLI fallback state machine.

capability_operations.signal: Compatibility manifest and dual host packaging
capability_operations.operation: Generate separately installable Codex and Claude artifacts with bundled runtime, canonical Skill, host metadata, and shared component digests.

capability_operations.signal: Protocol, fault, Skill-eval, host, update, uninstall, and platform verification
capability_operations.operation: Produce surface-specific automated and real-host evidence without unsupported readiness roll-up.
Future adoption.apply must consume a separately reviewed Kernel plan digest, revalidate it, and return before any task start.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: The user requires adoption writes in the first release.

capability_operations.operation: Future medium-risk execution requires declared client elicitation, human-response evidence, digest binding, and execution-time revalidation.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A target host proves a reliable portable human elicitation contract and first-release scope changes.

capability_operations.signal: Remote MCP, OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI, native packaging, and enterprise policy
capability_operations.operation: No first-release implementation or readiness claim; each surface requires a later specification and evidence boundary.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: Any listed surface becomes a required delivery outcome.

capability_operations.signal: High/destructive public MCP, force-stop, project removal, risky clean, reset, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force, and permanent follow
capability_operations.operation: The public Agent surface must not expose these operations or inputs.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A new reviewed safety contract explicitly adds one of these capabilities.

capability_operations.operation: Unknown or external-owned processes remain inspect-only.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: Ownership semantics are explicitly redefined in a new discussion.

capability_operations.operation: Use resource-aware idempotency, journal recovery, and explicit indeterminate outcomes instead.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: A restricted operation class can prove stronger semantics without changing the general promise.

capability_operations.operation: The first-release local control plane must not target production or remote managed services.
capability_operations.reopen_trigger: The user requires production or remote service management under a new trust, authentication, policy, and evidence contract.

result_contract.compatibility_rule: The CLI adapter may render or nest Agent result data inside the existing schemaVersion 1 envelope, but must not change the meaning of existing public fields.

journal_contract.location: A versioned user-scoped namespace under LAUNCHDECK_HOME shared by CLI, MCP, and Plugins
journal_contract.operation_id: Kernel-issued, immutable, and journaled in prepared state before any side effect; callers may supply a known ID only to retrieve or reconcile the original operation, never to nominate a new operation identity
journal_contract.unresolved_retention: prepared, running, and indeterminate records are not age-pruned; reconciliation or explicit safe administrative resolution is required
journal_contract.lost_response_lookup: If the response carrying the operation ID is lost, operation.list performs a bounded read filtered by projectRef, taskRef, operationName, createdSince, and journal state. A unique candidate can then be inspected/reconciled by ID; zero or multiple candidates return an explicit no-match/ambiguous result and never authorize a retry.
journal_contract.expired_recovery_behavior: A supplied recovery operation ID without a retained record returns operation_record_missing_or_expired and never starts new work
journal_contract.retry_rule: Same operation ID plus same input digest returns or recovers the original operation; a different digest is refused; an indeterminate result is inspect/reconcile only.

skill_migration_contract.detection: Installer and doctor inspect both locations and Plugin-bundled copies and compare a canonical content-manifest digest plus build identity.
skill_migration_contract.identical_duplicate: Report both locations, select the host-preferred location deterministically, and offer a user-controlled cleanup instruction without deleting automatically.
skill_migration_contract.divergent_duplicate: Report ambiguity and both digests, preserve every user file, and require an explicit user-selected migration or removal before claiming a single active Skill.
skill_migration_contract.new_install: Write only to the current preferred location when absent; an identical target is idempotent; divergent content is never overwritten.
skill_migration_contract.plugin_copy: A Plugin-bundled Skill is version-coupled to its bundled runtime and remains distinct from user-level installation while duplicate/drift diagnostics stay visible.

public_operation_contract.input_boundary: Declared project/task references, operation IDs, plan digests, cursors, limits, and bounded operation-specific options only
public_operation_contract.risk_boundary: Mutation operations execute only when the Kernel resolves the current configured risk to low and all ownership, scope, compatibility, and lock checks pass
public_operation_contract.observation_boundary: logs and events require a bounded limit/window/cursor and never provide permanent follow
public_operation_contract.stdout_boundary: The MCP runtime writes protocol frames only to stdout; diagnostics and incidental process output use stderr or bounded Launchdeck log/event state.

design_contract.surface_type: CLI, Skill, MCP protocol, and Plugin packaging behavior
design_contract.subject: Local project lifecycle control
design_contract.single_job: Express a lifecycle intent and receive a safe, observable, recoverable Launchdeck result without choosing unsafe low-level process controls.
design_contract.content_thesis: Reports name the project, task, executing surface/runtime, outcome, resource status, effects certainty, evidence, refusal reason, and safe next action.
design_contract.interaction_thesis: Observe before mutate; reuse matching runs; stop on refusal or uncertainty; reconcile before any retry.
experience_requirements: Users express lifecycle intent without naming low-level Launchdeck tools.
Agents observe before mutation and report an existing run instead of duplicating it.
Failures identify project, task, runtime/surface, evidence, refusal reason, and a safe next action.
Plugin and host lifecycle never unexpectedly stops a managed local service or deletes recovery evidence.
must_preserve: Structured error and safe next-action semantics
may_adapt: MCP host-specific tool presentation and manifest metadata
must_not: Imply deferred UI/TUI or remote control surfaces

minimal_live_reads: git status --short --branch confirmed that current dirty paths do not modify the runtime evidence paths retained by the validated discussion contract
validation_routes: Existing targeted CLI/installer/control-plane contract test command retained from the discussion evidence
Real stdio MCP protocol client suite
Mutation fault-injection and journal recovery suite
Separate real Codex and Claude host install/update/uninstall smokes
Surface-specific Windows, macOS, and Linux readiness matrix
stale_if: The source discussion review digest changes
Any retained live evidence path changes before planning
The implementation target moves outside F:/github/launchdeck
A target host cannot launch the bundled runtime under Node.js 20+ and the generated manifest model
A deferred surface becomes first-release scope
context_capsule.cognition_advisory: The upstream discussion recorded project cognition as unavailable and completed a live-repository truth pass. Compile mode reuses that current evidence and does not treat graph state as proof.

dispatch.purpose: Condition-triggered high-risk semantic-drift, protected-obligation, and artifact-consistency review before planning handoff.

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Return the existing run with reusedExistingRun=true; do not spawn.
Permit inspection only and refuse mutation.
Return possible or unknown effects certainty and require operation.get or operation.reconcile; never retry automatically.

state_behavior_matrix.state: response lost before the caller observes the Kernel-issued ID
state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Use bounded operation.list correlation by project/task/name/time/state; reconcile only one unique candidate and stop on zero or ambiguous matches.
Return or recover the original operation without re-execution.

state_behavior_matrix.state: same ID, different digest or missing retained record

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Refuse execution and return a new plan requirement.
Managed services and shared state remain; compatible standalone CLI can inspect and control them.
Preserve all content, report both digests, and require explicit user-selected migration.
Allow safe diagnosis/inspection where possible and fail writes closed.

dependency_impact.dependency: Registry, run index, locks, ownership, events, logs, runtime state, process and port adapters
dependency_impact.impact: Kernel extraction must reuse their truth and preserve daemonless lifecycle semantics.
dependency_impact.validation: Existing and new control-plane contract tests plus adapter-equivalence cases.

dependency_impact.impact: Kernel and normalized Agent results cannot break existing JSON automation.
dependency_impact.validation: Backward-compatibility fixtures and semantic mapping tests.

dependency_impact.dependency: MCP stdio framing and structured results
dependency_impact.impact: Non-protocol stdout, unbounded calls, or schema drift breaks every host.

dependency_impact.dependency: Codex and Claude host path resolution
dependency_impact.impact: One host manifest or path assumption cannot prove the other host works.
dependency_impact.validation: Separate real-host install, routing, update, disable/uninstall, and shared-state smokes.

dependency_impact.impact: Skill/runtime/schema drift can issue unsupported or unsafe operations.

recovery_and_validation: Locks and operation IDs make safe retries resource-aware and idempotent; they do not imply universal exactly-once execution.
Transport failure returns a recoverable known result or an explicit indeterminate result backed by journal and live evidence.
Rollback never terminates unknown/external processes or deletes shared state as a side effect of Plugin lifecycle.
Journal, compatibility, and manifest migrations must be versioned, fail writes closed on incompatibility, and retain diagnosis paths.
Acceptance evidence must include positive behavior, refusal behavior, interruption/restart, concurrency, update/uninstall, and takeover/recovery.

open_items.routing: Continue current workflow; preserve deferred confirmation contract.

open_items.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host cannot launch the bundled runtime without install scripts or a global CLI.
open_items.routing: Continue to planning with separate real-host verification tasks.

open_items.stop_and_reopen_condition: Module placement requires a second state authority or incompatible package boundary.
open_items.routing: Resolve final Kernel, MCP adapter/bundle, Plugin source/generation, and schema paths from live repository ownership.

open_items.stop_and_reopen_condition: Fresh live repository evidence contradicts the retained discussion evidence.
open_items.routing: Project cognition is route-only and currently unavailable/stale; use bounded live evidence for planning claims.

required_refs.purpose: User-confirmed upstream requirement and protected-obligation authority
Semantic terms, capability dispositions, and compile-mode delta audit
Affected objects, state behavior, dependency propagation, and planning evidence routes
